description: WORKED EXAMPLE (not a default) — the three real rules agent-guard was born from, in a private GTM automation repo. Each was derived from cc-logger telemetry (~3wk / 37k tool calls): a recurring failure became a rule, and the failure count is recorded in meta.why. This is what your own starter.rules.json should look like after a few passes of bin/derive_rules.py against your telemetry. Point AGENT_GUARD_RULES at a file like this, or merge the rules you want into rules/starter.rules.json.

rules.message: Neon MCP is connected to the wrong account and fails ~half the time. Use the sanctioned DB paths instead (project report CLI / typed db helper / a psql wrapper that clears libpq env).

Bare `psql` with no connection target falls back to the local socket and fails. Use the repo's psql wrapper, pass a full postgresql:// URL, or prefix PGDATABASE='' PGHOST='' PGPORT='' PGUSER='' before psql.

meta.why: ~18 logged failures: bare psql -> local socket.

rules.message: Don't shell-source .env — DSN values contain '&', so source/xargs word-splitting breaks them. Read env in Python (python-dotenv or a registry helper), or take the DSN straight from a helper CLI.
